71: This field focuses on treating and preventing diseases through proper nutrition. Clinical nutritionists work in hospitals, clinics, and private practices, helping patients with conditions such as diabetes, heart disease, eating disorders, and more.
73: Focuses on the specific nutritional needs of children from birth through adolescence. Pediatric dietitians work with parents and caregivers to ensure that children receive the nutrients they need to grow and develop properly.
75: It involves working at the community level to promote healthy eating habits and prevent diet-related diseases. This may include nutrition education programs in schools, workplaces, or underserved communities.
77: This type of nutrition is related to optimizing physical performance and recovery in athletes. It involves planning specific diets to improve sports performance, muscle mass gain, fat loss and recovery after exercise.
79: This field of nutrition focuses on preventing and treating obesity through diet and healthy lifestyle choices. Obesity dietitians work with patients to develop eating plans that promote sustainable, healthy weight loss. This typically involves reducing calorie intake, increasing physical activity, and adopting balanced, nutritious eating habits. They can also help patients identify and overcome psychological and behavioral factors that contribute to weight gain.

92: Discover the Story behind Our Journey in Nutrition!
95: On February 22, 2024, at FATEC, five students — Lucas, Vitor, Raiane, Ana Maria and Vinicius — met to discuss their academic and personal experiences. The conversation soon turned into a debate about the difficulties in finding reliable information about nutrition. Each shared frustrations about food labels and food myths, raising a collective awareness about the importance of healthy eating.
96: This exchange of ideas revealed a gap in the market: the need for a resource that combined scientific data with accessible language. Thus, NutriVida was born, an educational website designed to inform and inspire changes in eating behavior, promoting a healthier lifestyle. The team was determined to turn their passion for nutrition into an impactful project for other people's lives.
98: For weeks, the team immersed themselves in a true immersion in the science of nutrition. They participated in webinars, attended lectures and got involved in discussions in specialized forums. Lucas highlighted the importance of micronutrients; Vitor explored the bases of popular diets; Raiane focused on best nutrition practices, while Ana Maria studied the relationship between food and mental health. Vinicius, with his eye on design, sought to understand how to visually present information in an attractive and clear way. The meetings became dynamic, full of debates and insights. They not only collected data, but also discussed how to present this information in an engaging way. Ideas for the site's content began to take shape, and enthusiasm grew as they realized the potential of what they were creating.
100: With a solid collection of information, the team moved on to the planning phase. Raiane, as group manager, coordinated the discussions, ensuring that all points of view were heard. Together, they defined the structure of the site, deciding that each section would cover different aspects of nutrition, such as balanced diets, healthy recipes and common myths.
101: Ana Maria, focused on SEO, proposed strategies to optimize the content and ensure that the website reached the target audience. Vinicius brought his ideas to an intuitive and attractive design, while Lucas and Vitor worked on the content, making the information accessible and interesting. They set clear goals: increase visibility in online searches and create an engaged community. Each member felt motivated, knowing that their individual skills contributed to a greater goal: transforming NutriVida into an indispensable resource for those seeking a healthier life.
103: Determined to positively impact people's lives, the group defined that NutriVida would not just be a repository of information, but a practical tool that would allow each user to receive a specific menu based on their needs and preferences. Through interactive forms, users could enter data such as health goals, dietary restrictions and personal preferences.
104: The goal was to provide a personalized experience that helps each individual achieve their nutritional goals in an effective and sustainable way. Additionally, they wanted to create an inclusive community where people can share their stories, exchange tips, and support each other on their health journeys. NutriVida aimed to demystify nutrition, break stigmas associated with diets and promote a balanced and healthy approach to food, making everyone feel empowered to take care of their health.
106: Lucas was responsible for programming and marketing, creating an intuitive interface that facilitated navigation. He developed effective digital campaigns to promote Nutrivida to a wide audience.
107: Vitor produced enlightening content, demystifying erroneous concepts about nutrition based on reliable sources.
108: Raiane atuou como gestora do projeto, organizando tarefas e prazos para garantir uma colaboração fluida.
109: Ana Maria focused on SEO and accessibility, ensuring that the site was easily found and usable by everyone, including people with disabilities.
110: Vinicius created eye-catching infographics and illustrations, making the site's visual presentation engaging and informative.
111: During the structuring of the project, the team realized that the number of forms could make users feel overwhelmed and abandon the platform. To solve this, they decided to gamify the experience, transforming filling out forms into an interactive game.
113: One of the team's biggest challenges was the complexity of the forms needed for personalized nutritional recommendations. Raiane warned: If it's too long, no one will want to fill it out. Lucas suggested gamifying the process, making it lighter and more interactive, where users would earn small rewards as they progressed, but this brought technical challenges.
114: Furthermore, the team developed artificial intelligence to generate personalized menus. Lucas and Raiane collaborated on the algorithm, while Ana Maria ensured that gamification and AI were accessible to everyone. Vitor created content that could be unlocked during completion, and Vinicius designed motivating graphics to show users' progress.
115: After many tests and adjustments, the team overcame the challenges, making the Nutrivida experience not only informative, but also interactive and useful.
117: With the launch of the website, the Nutrivida team was excited about the positive impact of the solutions, seeing surprising engagement on forms and good reception of menu recommendations. However, they knew the work was just beginning.
118: Lucas planned to integrate a section of interactive recipes that would adapt to the menus, while Raiane dreamed of an online community for users to share experiences and recipes. Ana Maria sought to ensure the site's accessibility, planning partnerships to expand its reach. Vitor and Vinicius also considered visual and content improvements.
119: The team was excited about the possibilities of evolving Nutrivida and impacting even more lives through nutrition. With a clear vision and an unwavering commitment to quality, they were ready to face the next challenges and transform Nutrivida into a reference in the world of online nutrition.
